findings.evidence: Lines 114/119/123 use `exact congrArg Fin.val ...`; the PR adds `@[simp] theorem equivPerm_symm_apply_apply (σ) (c) : ((equivPerm n).symm σ : GridState n) c = σ c := rfl` (Diagram.lean:76-77) precisely to name this reduction, but the proof does not use it.
findings.fix: Rewrite the RHS explicitly with the simp lemmas this PR introduces, e.g. `simp only [equivPerm_symm_apply_apply]` (giving `(1 : Perm) c` / `Equiv.swap 0 1 c`) before discharging with the hypothesis via `Equiv.Perm.one_apply`/`Equiv.swap_apply_*`, instead of `congrArg Fin.val` + defeq. A uniform `simp [...]`/`omega`-on-`Fin.val` per case removes the reliance on accidental defeq.
findings.issue: In `eq_equivPerm_symm_one_or_eq_equivPerm_symm_swap`, after `ext c; fin_cases c` several cases are discharged with `exact congrArg Fin.val h1` (lines 114, 119, 123). These only typecheck because `((equivPerm 2).symm 1) c` and `((equivPerm 2).symm (Equiv.swap 0 1)) c` reduce by definitional equality through the `equivPerm` wrapper and the `CoeFun` coercion — exactly the undocumented cross-wrapper/coercion defeq the rubric flags. It is also inconsistent with the sibling `c = 0` cases (lines 111, 118) which use `simpa`.

summary: The cardinality proofs are robust (short named-lemma chain in `card`, `simp` for the small cases, `Subsingleton.elim` for uniqueness), and the new `equivPerm_*` rfl-lemmas correctly name the wrapper defeq. But the size-2 classification proof closes goals via `congrArg Fin.val` relying on undocumented defeq through the `equivPerm` wrapper and `CoeFun` coercion, instead of using the very simp lemmas this PR adds.
